issue.message: Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
issue.errorLine1: ip = if (ipAddr != 0) String.format("%d.%d.%d.%d", (ipAddr and 0xff), (ipAddr shr 8 and 0xff), (ipAddr shr 16 and 0xff), (ipAddr shr 24 and 0xff)) else null

issue.message: Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
issue.errorLine1: gateway = if (dhcp.gateway != 0) String.format("%d.%d.%d.%d", (dhcp.gateway and 0xff), (dhcp.gateway shr 8 and 0xff), (dhcp.gateway shr 16 and 0xff), (dhcp.gateway shr 24 and 0xff)) else null

issue.message: Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
issue.errorLine1: netmask = if (dhcp.netmask != 0) String.format("%d.%d.%d.%d", (dhcp.netmask and 0xff), (dhcp.netmask shr 8 and 0xff), (dhcp.netmask shr 16 and 0xff), (dhcp.netmask shr 24 and 0xff)) else null

issue.message: Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
issue.errorLine1: dns1 = if (dhcp.dns1 != 0) String.format("%d.%d.%d.%d", (dhcp.dns1 and 0xff), (dhcp.dns1 shr 8 and 0xff), (dhcp.dns1 shr 16 and 0xff), (dhcp.dns1 shr 24 and 0xff)) else null

issue.message: Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
issue.errorLine1: dns2 = if (dhcp.dns2 != 0) String.format("%d.%d.%d.%d", (dhcp.dns2 and 0xff), (dhcp.dns2 shr 8 and 0xff), (dhcp.dns2 shr 16 and 0xff), (dhcp.dns2 shr 24 and 0xff)) else null

issue.message: Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
issue.errorLine1: dhcpServer = if (dhcp.serverAddress != 0) String.format("%d.%d.%d.%d", (dhcp.serverAddress and 0xff), (dhcp.serverAddress shr 8 and 0xff), (dhcp.serverAddress shr 16 and 0xff), (dhcp.serverAddress shr 24 and 0xff)) else null

issue.message: Field requires API level 33 (current min is 26): `android.content.Context#RECEIVER_EXPORTED`
Field requires API level 29 (current min is 26): `android.content.pm.PackageManager#FEATURE_IRIS`
Field requires API level 29 (current min is 26): `android.content.pm.PackageManager#FEATURE_FACE`
Field requires API level 34 (current min is 31): `android.content.pm.PackageManager#FEATURE_UWB`
The locale folder "`id`" should be called "`in`" instead; see the `java.util.Locale` documentation
Call requires API level 29 (current min is 26): `android.hardware.camera2.CameraCharacteristics.Key()`
Not targeting the latest versions of Android; compatibility modes apply. Consider testing and updating this version. Consult the `android.os.Build.VERSION_CODES` javadoc for details.
Accessing internal APIs via reflection is not supported and may not work on all devices or in the future
Accessing internal APIs via reflection is not supported and may not work on all devices or in the future
Accessing internal APIs via reflection is not supported and may not work on all devices or in the future
Windows file separators (`\`) and drive letter separators (':') must be escaped (`\\`) in property files; use C\\:\\\\Program Files\\\\Android\\\\openjdk\\\\jdk-21.0.8
Attribute `localeConfig` is only used in API level 33 and higher (current min is 26)
Attribute `preferMinimalPostProcessing` is only used in API level 30 and higher (current min is 26)
Most apps don't need access to high sensor sampling rate.
As of Android 11, this method no longer returns information about all apps; see https://g.co/dev/packagevisibility for details
As of Android 11, this method no longer returns information about all apps; see https://g.co/dev/packagevisibility for details
As of Android 11, this method no longer returns information about all apps; see https://g.co/dev/packagevisibility for details
As of Android 11, this method no longer returns information about all apps; see https://g.co/dev/packagevisibility for details
Redundant label can be removed

Modifier parameter should be the first optional parameter

Use of this function is discouraged because resource reflection makes it harder to perform build optimizations and compile-time verification of code. It is much more efficient to retrieve resources by identifier (e.g. `R.foo.bar`) than by name (e.g. `getIdentifier("bar", "foo", null)`).

Use of this function is discouraged because resource reflection makes it harder to perform build optimizations and compile-time verification of code. It is much more efficient to retrieve resources by identifier (e.g. `R.foo.bar`) than by name (e.g. `getIdentifier("bar", "foo", null)`).
Suspicious argument type for formatting argument #1 in `temp_label`: conversion is `s`, received `float` (argument #2 in method call) (Did you mean formatting character `e`, 'f', 'g' or `a`?)

"Camera" is a common misspelling; did you mean "Câmera"?
issue.errorLine1: <string name="camera_permission_msg_1">- O aplicativo detecta a resolução real do sensor (antes do binning de pixels) usando o banco de dados do sensor e a API Camera2.</string>

issue.message: "Camera" is a common misspelling; did you mean "Câmera"?
issue.errorLine1: <string name="camera_disclaimer">Haverá inconsistência entre a resolução do sensor aqui e a resolução real fornecida pelos fabricantes devido à limitação da API Android Camera2. Verifique o fabricante para mais detalhes.</string>

issue.message: "video" is a common misspelling; did you mean "vídeo"?
"Camera" is a common misspelling; did you mean "Câmera"?
"Interface" is a common misspelling; did you mean "Interfaz"?
"Video" is a common misspelling; did you mean "Vídeo"?
Formatting %d followed by words ("Operator"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string

issue.message: Prefer `mutableDoubleStateOf` instead of `mutableStateOf`
Prefer `mutableFloatStateOf` instead of `mutableStateOf`
Prefer `mutableFloatStateOf` instead of `mutableStateOf`
Prefer `mutableFloatStateOf` instead of `mutableStateOf`

Prefer `mutableIntStateOf` instead of `mutableStateOf`
Prefer `mutableFloatStateOf` instead of `mutableStateOf`
Prefer `mutableFloatStateOf` instead of `mutableStateOf`
Prefer `mutableIntStateOf` instead of `mutableStateOf`
Set `android:baselineAligned="false"` on this element for better performance
Set `android:baselineAligned="false"` on this element for better performance
Set `android:baselineAligned="false"` on this element for better performance
Set `android:baselineAligned="false"` on this element for better performance
Set `android:baselineAligned="false"` on this element for better performance
Set `android:baselineAligned="false"` on this element for better performance
Nested weights are bad for performance
Nested weights are bad for performance
Nested weights are bad for performance
Nested weights are bad for performance
Possible overdraw: Root element paints background `?android:attr/colorBackground` with a theme that also paints a background (inferred theme is `@style/Theme.Relab_tool`)
